context_prose: E10 prepare skill is a markdown skill body Claude Code loads on /prepare or auto-trigger before new work begins. It contains no executable code: the body is a three-step instruction sequence the agent follows in its own context. Step 1 tells the agent to analyze the current conversation for what work is about to happen. Step 2 tells the agent to issue 2–3 targeted `engram recall --query "<topic>"` invocations described by task (not by fear). Step 3 tells the agent to summarize results back to the user. The skill's only outbound dependency is R2 (prepare skill → engram CLI binary, E9): the body's example bash lines instruct the agent to spawn `engram recall` subprocesses. R1 (Claude Code → Skills) is the inbound load edge from E3. The skill has no DI surface and no Go code — its "contract" is its prose, and every property below is enforced by the SKILL.md text itself.

edges.label: instructs agent to call engram recall --query

legend_items: **Component** — light blue (sibling skills not shown; E9 engram CLI binary as call target).
**R-edges** — solid; no DI back-edges (the skill is pure markdown — no Go DI surface).
name: prepare-skill

properties.name: Skill name matches directory
properties.notes: Architectural: Claude Code routes /prepare to the skill whose front-matter name is `prepare`. Mismatch breaks the slash-command binding.
properties.statement: For all loads of this skill by Claude Code, the YAML front-matter `name` field equals `prepare`, matching the parent directory name `skills/prepare/` and the slash-command `/prepare`.

properties.name: Trigger description names work-start situations
properties.notes: Architectural: skill triggering is description-driven; vague triggers fail to fire at the intended boundaries.
properties.statement: For all readers of the front-matter `description`, the text enumerates concrete work-start situations (starting new work, switching tasks, beginning a feature, changing direction, tackling an issue) so Claude Code's auto-trigger heuristic fires before — not after — implementation effort.

properties.name: Three-step flow shape
properties.notes: Order matters: analysis must precede query construction, and queries must precede the briefing.
properties.statement: For all invocations of /prepare, the body presents exactly three flow steps in order: (1) analyze the situation, (2) make targeted recall queries, (3) present briefing to user.

properties.name: Bounded query count
properties.notes: Bounds prevent both no-context starts and context-budget exhaustion before work begins.
properties.statement: For all invocations following the skill, Step 2 instructs the agent to issue between 2 and 3 targeted `engram recall` queries — not zero, not a flood.

properties.name: Recall invocation form
properties.notes: Drift here would teach the agent to call a non-existent flag or subcommand. Mirrors R2 in c3-skills.md.
properties.statement: For all example query lines in Step 2, the bash command shape is `engram recall --query "<topic>"` (single subcommand `recall` with the `--query` flag), matching the engram CLI binary's documented recall surface.

properties.name: Query-by-task discipline
properties.notes: Memory situations are stored task-shaped; fear-shaped queries miss because the cosine match space disagrees.
properties.statement: For all guidance about query phrasing, the skill instructs the agent to query by the task being undertaken ("what are you trying to do") rather than by anticipated failure modes ("what might go wrong").

properties.name: Positive and negative examples present
properties.notes: Pairing concrete dos with a contrasting don't materially raises adherence vs. abstract guidance alone.
properties.statement: For all readers, the skill provides both positive query examples (e.g., "implementing Claude Code hooks", "writing Go tests in [domain]", "git push workflow") and at least one DON'T example demonstrating the failure mode ("common mistakes when writing hooks").

properties.name: Briefing surfaces context to user
properties.notes: Without an explicit user-facing briefing the user has no chance to correct trajectory before implementation begins.
properties.statement: For all invocations, Step 3 instructs the agent to summarize the relevant recalled context and memories back to the user before proceeding with the work itself.

properties.name: No direct I/O in skill body
properties.notes: Architectural: matches the project DI principle (skills are behavior, the binary owns I/O). Any future drift here would create a second I/O surface to keep in sync.
properties.statement: For all behaviors prescribed by this skill, no step instructs the agent to read or write filesystem state, network endpoints, or memory storage directly — every external effect routes through the engram CLI binary (E9) via R2.

properties.name: Read-only with respect to memory store
properties.notes: Mutation belongs to remember/learn/migrate skills (E11/E13/E14). Prepare is a read-only context-loading boundary.
properties.statement: For all invocations following the skill, the only engram subcommand invoked is `recall`; the skill never instructs the agent to call `engram learn`, `engram update`, or any mutating subcommand.

properties.name: Front-matter parses as YAML
properties.notes: Architectural: Claude Code's skill loader requires valid YAML front-matter. Malformed YAML silently disables the skill.
properties.statement: For all loads of skills/prepare/SKILL.md by Claude Code, the leading `---`-delimited block parses as YAML and exposes both `name` and `description` keys.
